articles.headline: India Unveils National Green Hydrogen Mission's Phase II, Aims for Global Leadership in Clean Energy
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet on Thursday approved the second phase of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, focusing on scaling up production and reducing costs.
• Phase II targets significant investments in electrolyzer manufacturing, green hydrogen production, and demand creation across various sectors like refining, fertilizer, and steel.
• Key objectives include achieving 5 million metric tonnes (MMT) of green hydrogen production capacity annually by 2030 and creating 6 lakh jobs.
• The mission emphasizes research and development, pilot projects, and international collaborations to establish India as a hub for green hydrogen export.
• Financial incentives, including production-linked incentives (PLI) for electrolyzer manufacturing and green hydrogen production, are central to the strategy.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Investment models
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Environmental pollution and degradation
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission launched in January 2023.
Phase II focuses on scaling production, reducing costs, and boosting demand.
Target: 5 MMT green hydrogen production capacity annually by 2030.
Expected cumulative reduction in fossil fuel imports: over ₹1 lakh crore by 2030.
PLI schemes for electrolyzer manufacturing and green hydrogen production are key components.
mainsPoints: Significance of Green Hydrogen for India's energy security and climate goals.
Economic opportunities: job creation, investment, export potential.
Challenges in implementation: cost reduction, infrastructure development, technology absorption.
Role of government policies (PLI, R&D support) in fostering the ecosystem.
Impact on India's geopolitical standing in the global clean energy transition.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production plant with renewable energy sources.
articles.trivia: India aims to become a global hub for the production and export of green hydrogen, leveraging its abundant renewable energy resources.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Reinforces Call for Greater Transparency in Electoral Funding, Directs EC on Disclosure Norms
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a significant ruling, reiterated the imperative for enhanced transparency in political funding and directed the Election Commission of India (ECI) to ensure stricter disclosure norms for political parties.
• The judgment emphasized that the right to information of the voter extends to knowing the source of funding for political parties, crucial for a healthy democracy.
• While not overturning previous judgments on electoral bonds, the court urged the ECI to proactively publish data and investigate non-compliance with existing disclosure requirements.
• This ruling comes amidst ongoing debates about the opaqueness of electoral financing and its impact on free and fair elections.
• The court highlighted the need for legislative action to address the structural issues in electoral funding, while also leveraging existing legal frameworks for greater accountability.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
Role of Election Commission of India.
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court's role in electoral reforms.
Article 19(1)(a) - Right to freedom of speech and expression includes right to know.
Election Commission of India's mandate regarding political party funding.
Previous SC rulings on electoral bonds and their impact.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the impact of opaque political funding on democratic processes in India.
Discuss the role of the judiciary and Election Commission in ensuring transparency in electoral financing.
Suggest comprehensive reforms to strengthen accountability and transparency in political funding.
Examine the balance between donor privacy and voter's right to information in the context of electoral bonds.
articles.imageDescription: Image of the Supreme Court of India building.
articles.trivia: The first Lok Sabha elections in India were held from October 1951 to February 1952.

articles.headline: India-ASEAN Summit Concludes with Focus on Enhanced Connectivity, Digital Partnership, and Maritime Security
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Summit concluded in Jakarta, with leaders reaffirming their commitment to a comprehensive strategic partnership focusing on key areas.
• Discussions centered on bolstering physical and digital connectivity, including infrastructure projects and collaboration in digital public infrastructure.
• A joint statement highlighted renewed emphasis on maritime security cooperation, freedom of navigation, and adherence to international law in the Indo-Pacific region.
• Economic engagement was a major agenda point, with calls for boosting trade and investment, and exploring new avenues for supply chain resilience.
• India reiterated its 'Act East' policy and its vision for a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, aligning with ASEAN's centrality.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: ASEAN is a regional intergovernmental organization of ten Southeast Asian countries.
India became a Sectoral Dialogue Partner of ASEAN in 1992 and a Full Dialogue Partner in 1995.
The relationship was elevated to a Comprehensive Strategic Partnership in 2022.
Key pillars of cooperation: political-security, economic, and socio-cultural.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India's 'Act East' policy in the context of its engagement with ASEAN.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for deeper India-ASEAN cooperation in economic and strategic domains.
Evaluate India's role in promoting regional stability and a rules-based order in the Indo-Pacific.
Examine how enhanced connectivity and digital partnership can foster greater integration between India and Southeast Asia.
articles.imageDescription: Leaders from India and ASEAN nations shaking hands at a summit.
articles.trivia: ASEAN was founded on August 8, 1967, in Bangkok, Thailand, with five member countries.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate, Focuses on Inflation Management and Economic Stability
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) announced its bi-monthly review, deciding to maintain the repo rate at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive time.
• The decision aligns with the MPC's objective of anchoring inflation expectations while supporting economic growth amidst global uncertainties.
• RBI Governor highlighted concerns over volatile food prices and geopolitical risks, which continue to pose challenges to the inflation outlook.
• The central bank reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation progressively aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Experts anticipate continued vigilance from the RBI, with future policy actions contingent on evolving inflation trajectory and domestic economic indicators.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary policy.
prelimsPoints: Repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a six-member body responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate.
RBI's primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Inflation target set by the government for RBI is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in managing India's current economic landscape.
Examine the impact of global economic factors and geopolitical events on India's monetary policy decisions.
Evaluate the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance and its implications for credit growth and investment.
articles.imageDescription: Building of the Reserve Bank of India in Mumbai.
articles.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, based on the recommendations of the Hilton Young Commission.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite for Enhanced Remote Sensing Capabilities
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched an advanced Earth Observation Satellite (EOS-07) onboard the PSLV-C58 rocket from Sriharikota.
• The satellite is designed to provide high-resolution imagery for various applications including urban planning, disaster management, agriculture, and forestry.
• EOS-07 incorporates cutting-edge remote sensing instruments, promising improved data accuracy and faster turnaround times for critical information.
• This mission further strengthens India's indigenous capabilities in space-based earth observation and contributes to national development goals.
• The launch also carried several co-passenger satellites, including some from international partners, showcasing India's growing role in global space services.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: PSLV is ISRO's third-generation launch vehicle.
Earth Observation Satellites (EOS) are used for monitoring Earth's environment, climate, and resources.
Sriharikota is the primary spaceport of ISRO.
Applications of remote sensing: agriculture, forestry, geology, urban planning, disaster management.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Earth Observation Satellites for India's national security and development.
Analyze how ISRO's advancements in space technology contribute to various sectors like agriculture, disaster management, and urban planning.
Examine the role of indigenous space capabilities in achieving self-reliance (Atmanirbhar Bharat) in critical technological domains.
Evaluate the potential for international collaboration and commercial opportunities arising from India's space program.
articles.imageDescription: A PSLV rocket lifting off from the launch pad.
articles.trivia: ISRO was formed in 1969, succeeding the Indian National Committee for Space Research (INCOSPAR).

articles.headline: Ministry of Environment Releases Revised Coastal Zone Management Plan for Sustainable Development
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) unveiled a revised Coastal Zone Management Plan (CZMP), aiming to balance conservation with sustainable development along India's vast coastline.
• The new plan incorporates lessons from recent extreme weather events and integrates climate change adaptation strategies, focusing on resilience of coastal communities.
• Key provisions include stricter regulations for construction in ecologically sensitive areas, promotion of blue economy activities, and participatory approaches involving local communities.
• The CZMP also streamlines environmental clearances for permissible activities while enhancing protection for mangroves, coral reefs, and other critical coastal ecosystems.
• It seeks to address challenges posed by sea-level rise, coastal erosion, and pollution, ensuring ecological integrity and livelihood security for coastal populations.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
prelimsPoints: Coastal Regulation Zone (CRZ) Notification, 2018, forms the basis for CZMP.
CZMP categorizes coastal areas based on ecological sensitivity and development potential.
Blue Economy refers to sustainable use of ocean resources for economic growth, improved livelihoods, and ocean ecosystem health.
Mangroves act as natural barriers against cyclones and tsunamis.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges in balancing environmental protection and developmental needs along India's coastline.
Analyze how the revised Coastal Zone Management Plan addresses the impacts of climate change and promotes resilience.
Examine the role of local communities and participatory governance in effective coastal zone management.
Evaluate the potential of the 'Blue Economy' concept in achieving sustainable development goals for coastal regions.
articles.imageDescription: A serene coastal landscape with mangroves and a calm sea.
articles.trivia: India has a coastline of over 7,500 km, including the mainland and island territories.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'Nari Shakti Udyam Yojana' to Boost Women Entrepreneurship and Self-Reliance
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development, in collaboration with SIDBI, launched the 'Nari Shakti Udyam Yojana', a new scheme aimed at empowering women entrepreneurs across India.
• The scheme provides financial assistance, mentorship, skill development training, and market linkages to women-led micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSMEs).
• It focuses on promoting entrepreneurship in both traditional and non-traditional sectors, with a special emphasis on digital literacy and e-commerce platforms.
• The initiative seeks to address gender disparities in business ownership and contribute to women's economic independence and overall societal development.
• Beneficiaries will receive subsidized loans, incubation support, and access to a dedicated online portal for resources and networking.
gsSubTopics: Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
Social empowerment.
prelimsPoints: SIDBI (Small Industries Development Bank of India) is the principal financial institution for promotion, financing and development of MSMEs.
The scheme provides financial and non-financial support.
Focus on reducing gender gap in entrepreneurship.
Aims to leverage technology for market access.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of women entrepreneurship for India's economic growth and social development.
Analyze the barriers faced by women in starting and scaling businesses in India.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government schemes like 'Nari Shakti Udyam Yojana' in promoting women's economic empowerment.
Suggest further policy measures to foster a more inclusive entrepreneurial ecosystem for women.
articles.imageDescription: A group of women entrepreneurs working together in an office setting.
articles.trivia: According to the 6th Economic Census (2013-14), women constitute around 13.76% of the total entrepreneurs in India.

articles.headline: India Bolsters Cyber Security Framework with New Guidelines for Critical Information Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• The National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC) and CERT-In released updated guidelines for safeguarding India's Critical Information Infrastructure (CII).
• The new framework mandates enhanced security protocols, regular audits, and incident response mechanisms for sectors like banking, power, telecom, and transportation.
• It emphasizes a multi-stakeholder approach, encouraging greater collaboration between government agencies, private sector entities, and cybersecurity experts.
• The guidelines aim to counter the evolving threat landscape, including state-sponsored attacks, ransomware, and data breaches, ensuring national security and economic stability.
• Capacity building, skill development, and indigenous cybersecurity solutions are also integral components of the updated policy.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
prelimsPoints: NCIIPC protects Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) from cyber threats.
CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the national agency for responding to computer security incidents.
CII sectors include power, banking, telecom, transport, government, and strategic public enterprises.
Information Technology Act, 2000, provides legal framework for cyber security.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing threats to India's cyber security and its implications for national security and economic stability.
Analyze the effectiveness of existing cyber security frameworks and policies in protecting critical infrastructure.
Suggest measures to enhance India's cyber resilience, including technological advancements, international cooperation, and capacity building.
Examine the challenges in implementing a robust cybersecurity framework in a diverse and rapidly digitizing economy like India.
articles.imageDescription: Digital representation of a secure network with interconnected nodes.
articles.trivia: India is one of the top countries experiencing a high volume of cyberattacks globally.

articles.headline: Inter-State Council Meeting Agenda Focuses on Cooperative Federalism and Harmonization of Policies
articles.explainer:
• The Inter-State Council convened a crucial meeting, with its agenda primarily focusing on strengthening cooperative federalism and achieving policy harmonization across states.
• Key discussions included the implementation of uniform education policies, health infrastructure development, and efficient disaster management strategies.
• The meeting aimed to foster better coordination between the Union and State governments on various legislative and administrative matters.
• States raised concerns regarding fiscal federalism, resource allocation, and the need for greater autonomy in certain policy domains.
• Recommendations are expected to be forwarded to the Union government to address inter-state disputes and streamline the implementation of national programs.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
prelimsPoints: Inter-State Council is a non-permanent constitutional body established under Article 263 of the Indian Constitution.
It is constituted by the President of India.

Its function is to inquire into and advise upon disputes between states, investigate and discuss subjects in which some or all of the states, or the Union and one or more of the states, have a common interest.
The Prime Minister is the Chairman of the Inter-State Council.
mainsPoints: Analyze the role and significance of the Inter-State Council in promoting cooperative federalism in India.
Discuss the major challenges in Centre-State relations that necessitate the functioning of bodies like the Inter-State Council.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the Inter-State Council in resolving inter-state disputes and harmonizing policies.
Suggest reforms to strengthen the Inter-State Council and enhance its capacity to foster collaborative governance.
articles.imageDescription: A formal meeting table with national and state flags.
articles.trivia: The Inter-State Council was first established in 1990 on the recommendation of the Sarkaria Commission.

articles.headline: Debate Intensifies on MSP Reforms: Government Explores New Models for Farmer Income Security
articles.explainer:
• Discussions around Minimum Support Price (MSP) reforms gained momentum, with the government indicating a willingness to explore new models for ensuring farmer income security.
• The ongoing debate involves various stakeholders, including farmer unions, agricultural economists, and state governments, on the viability and fiscal implications of legalizing MSP.
• Proposals include moving towards a 'price deficiency payment' system or a 'market assurance scheme' to protect farmers without distorting market dynamics.
• The government aims to create a sustainable framework that provides fair prices to farmers while also managing food inflation and public procurement costs.
• Expert committees are reportedly studying successful models from other countries and evaluating their applicability to India's diverse agricultural landscape.
gsSubTopics: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System- objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
keyTerms: Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP)
prelimsPoints: MSP is a form of market intervention by the Government of India to protect agricultural producers against any sharp fall in farm prices.
CACP recommends MSPs for 22 mandated crops and fair and remunerative price (FRP) for sugarcane.
MSP is announced by the Government of India at the beginning of the sowing season for certain crops.
The system aims to ensure remunerative prices for farmers and incentivize higher production.
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind Minimum Support Price (MSP) and its role in ensuring farmer welfare and food security in India.
Analyze the economic and fiscal implications of legally guaranteeing MSP for all crops.
Evaluate alternative models like price deficiency payments or market assurance schemes as solutions for farmer income security.
Examine the challenges associated with the current MSP regime, including market distortions and procurement inefficiencies.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer standing in a lush green field.
articles.trivia: The concept of MSP was introduced in India in the mid-1960s to support the Green Revolution.

articles.headline: India's Defense Exports Witness Significant Surge, Targeting ₹35,000 Crore by 2025
articles.explainer:
• India's defense exports have seen a remarkable increase, driven by the 'Make in India' initiative and efforts to boost indigenous manufacturing capabilities.
• The Ministry of Defence announced a target of ₹35,000 crore (USD 5 billion) in defense exports by 2025, showcasing India's growing role as a defense manufacturing hub.
• Key export items include missiles, artillery guns, radar systems, armored vehicles, and patrol vessels, primarily to friendly foreign countries in Asia, Africa, and the Middle East.
• This growth is attributed to policy reforms, ease of doing business, and the participation of private sector players in defense production.
• The increased exports not only contribute to economic growth but also strengthen India's strategic partnerships and diplomatic influence.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
prelimsPoints: India aims for ₹35,000 crore (USD 5 billion) in defense exports by 2025.
The 'Make in India' initiative is a major driver for defense indigenization.
Defense Public Sector Undertakings (DPSUs) and private players contribute to exports.
Exports are primarily to friendly foreign nations.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to the recent surge in India's defense exports and its implications for the Indian economy.
Discuss how increased defense production and exports align with India's 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' vision and strategic autonomy.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India to establish itself as a significant global defense manufacturing and export hub.
Evaluate the role of policy reforms and private sector participation in boosting India's defense industrial base.
articles.imageDescription: Modern Indian-made military equipment being showcased.
articles.trivia: India is currently among the top global arms importers, but aims to become a net exporter of defense equipment.

articles.headline: Project Tiger Expands Scope to Include Wetland Conservation for Holistic Biodiversity Protection
articles.explainer:
• In a strategic move, Project Tiger, India's flagship tiger conservation program, has announced an expansion of its mandate to include the conservation of critical wetland ecosystems.
• This holistic approach recognizes the interconnectedness of tiger habitats with associated wetlands, which are crucial for prey species and overall ecological balance.
• The initiative will focus on restoring degraded wetlands within and around tiger reserves, improving water quality, and managing invasive species.
• Experts highlight that healthy wetlands support diverse biodiversity, acting as carbon sinks and mitigating climate change impacts, thereby indirectly benefiting tiger populations.
• Collaborative efforts with local communities and other conservation agencies will be central to the successful implementation of this expanded scope.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and its conservation.
prelimsPoints: Project Tiger was launched in 1973.
National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA) is the statutory body responsible for Project Tiger.
Wetlands are vital ecosystems providing numerous services, recognized by Ramsar Convention.
Tigers are apex predators and their conservation signifies the health of the entire ecosystem.
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind expanding the scope of Project Tiger to include wetland conservation.
Analyze the ecological significance of wetlands and their role in supporting tiger habitats and overall biodiversity.
Examine the challenges in integrated conservation efforts for large predators and their associated ecosystems.
Suggest strategies for effective implementation of this expanded mandate, including community involvement and inter-agency coordination.
articles.imageDescription: A tiger walking near a wetland area in a forest.
articles.trivia: India is home to over 70% of the world's tiger population.

articles.headline: India Achieves Milestone in Indigenous Vaccine Development with New Platform Technology
articles.explainer:
• Indian scientists have announced a significant breakthrough in indigenous vaccine development, unveiling a novel platform technology that promises faster and more cost-effective vaccine production.
• This new platform is designed to rapidly respond to emerging infectious diseases and can be adapted for various viral and bacterial pathogens.
• The development aligns with India's goal of achieving self-reliance in healthcare and enhancing its capacity to address public health emergencies.
• Funding from the Department of Biotechnology and collaboration between public and private research institutions were crucial for this achievement.
• The technology is expected to reduce India's reliance on foreign vaccine imports and strengthen its position as a global vaccine manufacturing hub.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health.
prelimsPoints: India is the largest producer of vaccines globally.
Department of Biotechnology (DBT) supports R&D in biotechnology.
Vaccine platform technology allows for rapid development of vaccines against new pathogens.
Self-reliance in vaccine production is crucial for health security.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of indigenous vaccine development for India's public health security and global health leadership.
Analyze how advancements in platform technologies can revolutionize vaccine production and pandemic preparedness.
Examine the role of government funding and public-private partnerships in fostering scientific innovation in healthcare.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India in becoming a global leader in vaccine research and manufacturing.
articles.imageDescription: Scientists working in a laboratory with vaccine vials.
articles.trivia: India's Universal Immunization Programme (UIP) is one of the largest public health programs in the world.

articles.headline: Government Submits New Nominations for UNESCO World Heritage Status, Focus on Cultural Landscapes
articles.explainer:
• India has submitted a fresh set of nominations to UNESCO for World Heritage Site status, with a particular focus on unique cultural landscapes and architectural marvels.
• The Ministry of Culture highlighted the emphasis on sites that represent India's diverse heritage and demonstrate outstanding universal value.
• The nomination process involves extensive documentation, scientific research, and community engagement to ensure the authenticity and integrity of the sites.
• These efforts aim to enhance global recognition of India's rich cultural legacy and boost tourism, contributing to local economies.
• The selection criteria often include cultural significance, natural beauty, and historical importance, reflecting a blend of tangible and intangible heritage.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
prelimsPoints: UNESCO World Heritage Sites are designated for having cultural, historical, scientific or other forms of significance.
India has both cultural and natural World Heritage Sites.
The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) is responsible for archaeological research and protection of cultural heritage.
Cultural landscapes are combined works of nature and man, illustrating the evolution of human society and settlement over time.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of UNESCO World Heritage status for the preservation and promotion of India's cultural and natural heritage.
Analyze the challenges involved in the nomination and maintenance of World Heritage Sites in India.
Examine how the focus on cultural landscapes in new nominations reflects an evolving understanding of heritage conservation.
Evaluate the economic and social benefits of heritage tourism for local communities and national development.
articles.imageDescription: An ancient Indian monument bathed in morning light.
articles.trivia: The first Indian sites to be inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage List were the Ajanta Caves, Ellora Caves, Agra Fort, and Taj Mahal in 1983.

articles.headline: Progress Report Highlights Accelerated Pace of Bharatmala Pariyojana and Sagarmala Programme
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Road Transport and Highways, along with the Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways, released a joint progress report on the Bharatmala Pariyojana and Sagarmala Programme.
• The report indicates an accelerated pace of project completion, particularly in economic corridors, inter-corridor routes, and port connectivity roads under Bharatmala.
• Under Sagarmala, significant strides have been made in port modernization, new port development, coastal community development, and inland waterways infrastructure.
• These mega infrastructure projects are crucial for improving logistics efficiency, reducing transportation costs, and boosting economic growth across various regions.
• Challenges related to land acquisition, environmental clearances, and funding remain, but efforts are underway to streamline processes and ensure timely execution.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models.
prelimsPoints: Bharatmala Pariyojana is a centrally-sponsored and funded road and highways project of the Government of India.
Sagarmala Programme aims to promote port-led development in India.
Both projects focus on improving connectivity and reducing logistics costs.
Multimodal transport is a key component of these initiatives.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of large-scale infrastructure projects like Bharatmala and Sagarmala for India's economic growth and competitiveness.
Analyze how improved connectivity through these programs can boost regional development and integrate remote areas into the national economy.
Examine the challenges encountered in the implementation of such mega projects, including land acquisition and environmental concerns.
Evaluate the potential of multimodal logistics to reduce India's logistics costs and enhance trade efficiency.
articles.imageDescription: A modern highway with multiple lanes and a port in the background.
articles.trivia: India aims to bring down its logistics cost from 13-14% of GDP to 8-10% through various infrastructure initiatives.

articles.headline: India Reiterates Call for UN Reforms and Global South Priorities at UN General Assembly Session
articles.explainer:
• India's Permanent Representative to the UN addressed the ongoing UN General Assembly (UNGA) session, reiterating India's consistent call for comprehensive reforms of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC).
• The address highlighted India's commitment to multilateralism and emphasized the need for the UN to reflect contemporary geopolitical realities, including greater representation for the Global South.
• Key priorities articulated included climate action, counter-terrorism, sustainable development goals (SDGs), and equitable access to resources and technology.
• India also underscored its contributions to UN peacekeeping operations and its role in fostering international cooperation on critical global challenges.
• The session saw India advocating for a stronger, reformed multilateral system capable of effectively addressing complex global issues.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora - their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: UNGA is the main deliberative, policymaking and representative organ of the UN.
UNSC has 5 permanent members (P5) and 10 non-permanent members.
India is a strong proponent of UNSC reforms, advocating for permanent membership.
Global South refers to developing countries in Asia, Africa, and Latin America.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's long-standing demand for UN Security Council reforms and its rationale.
Analyze the significance of India championing the priorities of the Global South at international fora like the UNGA.
Examine India's role and contributions to global governance and multilateral efforts to address critical challenges.
Evaluate the challenges in achieving comprehensive UN reforms and the path forward for India's diplomatic efforts.
articles.imageDescription: The United Nations headquarters building in New York.
articles.trivia: India has contributed over 275,000 troops to UN peacekeeping missions since the 1950s, more than any other country.

articles.headline: National Education Policy (NEP) Implementation Review: Focus on Foundational Literacy and Skill Integration
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education conducted a review of the implementation progress of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, focusing on key areas such as foundational literacy and numeracy (FLN) and skill integration.
• The review highlighted advancements in curriculum development, teacher training programs, and the establishment of new educational technologies.
• Significant attention was given to the 'NIPUN Bharat' mission, aimed at achieving universal foundational literacy and numeracy for all children by Grade 3.
• Challenges in infrastructure, digital divide, and capacity building for educators were also discussed, with strategies outlined for accelerated implementation.
• The policy aims to transform India's education system to meet the demands of the 21st century, promoting critical thinking, holistic development, and vocational skills.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the 1986 National Policy on Education.
It proposes a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure.
NIPUN Bharat Mission aims for FLN by 2026-27.
Emphasis on multidisciplinary and holistic education.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of the National Education Policy 2020 in addressing the challenges of India's education system.
Analyze the key initiatives and strategies under NEP, particularly 'NIPUN Bharat', for enhancing foundational learning outcomes.
Examine the challenges in the effective implementation of NEP, including resource allocation, teacher training, and technological integration.
Evaluate how NEP aims to foster a future-ready workforce through skill integration and vocational education.
articles.imageDescription: Students engaged in a classroom learning activity.
articles.trivia: The Kothari Commission (1964-66) was the first commission in India to recommend a uniform '10+2+3' system of education.

articles.headline: India-Pakistan Hold Talks on Indus Water Treaty Amidst Hydropower Project Concerns
articles.explainer:
• Officials from India and Pakistan held discussions under the framework of the Indus Water Treaty (IWT) to address ongoing concerns regarding various hydropower projects on the Western rivers.
• The meeting, facilitated by the Permanent Indus Commission, aimed at resolving technical disputes and ensuring adherence to the provisions of the 1960 treaty.
• India reiterated its stance on its right to utilize waters of the Western rivers for run-of-the-river projects, strictly within the treaty's specifications.
• Pakistan raised objections to specific design elements of certain Indian projects, citing potential impact on water flow to its territory.
• While no major breakthrough was reported, both sides emphasized the importance of continuing dialogue to maintain the spirit of the treaty, which has largely survived periods of strained relations.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: Indus Water Treaty (IWT) was signed in 1960 between India and Pakistan, brokered by the World Bank.

articles.headline: India's Digital Public Infrastructure Model Gains Global Traction, Enhancing Financial Inclusion Worldwide
articles.explainer:
• India's pioneering Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model, encompassing Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, is garnering significant international interest and adoption, particularly in developing economies.
• The model offers a scalable, interoperable, and cost-effective framework for digital identity, payments, and data exchange, fostering financial inclusion and efficient service delivery.
• Several countries are exploring collaborations with India to replicate aspects of its DPI stack, recognizing its potential to accelerate their digital transformation journeys.
• This global traction highlights India's leadership in digital governance and its role in promoting equitable access to digital services worldwide.
• Discussions at international forums are focusing on adapting India's experience to suit diverse national contexts and ensuring data privacy and security in these implementations.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
prelimsPoints: DPI refers to shared digital systems that enable public and private services.
Aadhaar provides a unique digital identity.
UPI is a real-time payment system developed by NPCI.
DigiLocker is a secure cloud-based platform for issuance and verification of documents.
India's DPI model is often referred to as the 'India Stack'.
mainsPoints: Discuss the foundational components of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) and their role in driving financial inclusion and digital transformation.
Analyze why India's DPI model is gaining global traction, particularly in developing countries, and its implications for international cooperation.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in replicating the India Stack in diverse national contexts, considering aspects like data privacy and regulatory frameworks.
Evaluate how India's leadership in DPI can contribute to achieving Sustainable Development Goals globally.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting interconnected digital services like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker.
articles.trivia: UPI processed over 10 billion transactions in August 2024, cementing its position as a leading digital payment system.

articles.headline: Nation Observes Gandhi Jayanti with Renewed Commitment to Swachh Bharat Abhiyan and Gandhian Ideals

• India commemorated the 155th birth anniversary of Mahatma Gandhi, with leaders and citizens reaffirming their commitment to his ideals of truth, non-violence, and cleanliness.
• Special emphasis was placed on the 'Swachh Bharat Abhiyan' (Clean India Mission), launched on Gandhi Jayanti in 2014, highlighting its achievements and future goals.
• Events across the country included cleanliness drives, peace marches, and discussions on Gandhian philosophy and its relevance in contemporary times.
• President and Prime Minister paid tributes to Mahatma Gandhi, underscoring his enduring legacy in shaping India's socio-political landscape.
• The occasion served as a reminder of the importance of community participation and individual responsibility in nation-building and upholding ethical values.

articles.headline: Government Expands Air Quality Monitoring Network in Urban Areas, Focus on Tier-2 Cities
articles.explainer:
• The Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) announced a significant expansion of India's air quality monitoring network, with a particular focus on Tier-2 cities and industrial clusters.
• This initiative aims to provide more granular and real-time data on ambient air quality, crucial for informed policy-making and public health interventions.
• The expansion includes the deployment of advanced continuous ambient air quality monitoring stations (CAAQMS) and integration with existing manual stations.
• Data from the expanded network will feed into the National Air Quality Index (NAQI) and provide critical insights into pollution sources and trends.
• This move underscores the government's commitment to tackling air pollution, a major environmental and health challenge across urban India.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
keyTerms: Continuous Ambient Air Quality Monitoring Stations (CAAQMS)
prelimsPoints: CPCB is a statutory organization under the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
NAQI provides real-time information on air quality to the public.
Key pollutants monitored include PM2.5, PM10, SO2, NO2, CO, O3, NH3, Pb.
Tier-2 cities are experiencing rapid urbanization and industrialization, leading to increased pollution.
mainsPoints: Discuss the severity of air pollution in Indian cities and its impact on public health and the environment.
Analyze the role of an expanded air quality monitoring network in developing effective pollution control strategies.
Examine the challenges faced by Tier-2 cities in managing air pollution amidst rapid urbanization and industrial growth.
Suggest comprehensive measures, including policy interventions and technological solutions, to improve urban air quality in India.

articles.headline: Delayed Monsoon Withdrawal Raises Concerns for Rabi Crop Sowing and Agricultural Output
articles.explainer:
• The delayed withdrawal of the Southwest Monsoon from several parts of India has raised concerns among agricultural experts and farmers, particularly for the upcoming Rabi crop sowing season.
• While some regions benefited from extended rainfall, others faced issues of waterlogging and potential damage to standing Kharif crops, impacting harvesting schedules.
• The India Meteorological Department (IMD) indicated that the delayed withdrawal is influenced by localized weather patterns and global climate phenomena.
• Farmers are closely monitoring soil moisture levels and preparing for the Rabi season, with potential shifts in crop choices or delayed sowing for wheat, mustard, and pulses.
• The government is assessing the situation to provide necessary advisories and support to farmers to mitigate any adverse impacts on agricultural output and food security.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of world’s physical geography.

Distribution of key natural resources across the world (including South Asia and the Indian subcontinent); factors responsible for the location of primary, secondary, and tertiary sector industries in various parts of the world (including India).

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
prelimsPoints: Rabi crops are sown in winter (October-December) and harvested in spring (April-June). E.g., wheat, barley, peas, gram, mustard.
Kharif crops are sown with the onset of monsoon (June-July) and harvested in September-October. E.g., rice, maize, jowar, bajra, cotton.
IMD is the principal agency for meteorological observations and weather forecasting in India.
Monsoon withdrawal typically begins in September from northwest India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the impact of climate change and erratic monsoon patterns on India's agricultural sector and food security.
Analyze how delayed monsoon withdrawal affects crop cycles, particularly the transition from Kharif to Rabi season.
Examine the strategies that farmers and the government can adopt to mitigate the risks associated with unpredictable weather events.
Evaluate the role of meteorological forecasting and agricultural advisories in enhancing farmers' resilience to climate variability.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer inspecting his fields after unexpected rainfall.
articles.trivia: The word 'monsoon' is derived from the Arabic word 'mausim', meaning season.

articles.headline: Largest Floating Solar Energy Park Inaugurated in Southern India, Boosting Renewable Capacity
articles.explainer:
• A major floating solar energy park, one of the largest in Southern India, was inaugurated today, significantly contributing to the region's renewable energy capacity.
• The project utilizes advanced photovoltaic technology deployed on a large reservoir, minimizing land usage and reducing water evaporation.
• This initiative aligns with India's ambitious targets for increasing its renewable energy footprint and achieving energy security.
• The park is expected to power thousands of households and industries, reducing reliance on fossil fuels and lowering carbon emissions.
• Public-private partnerships and innovative financing models were crucial for the successful development and timely completion of this large-scale green energy project.

Investment models.

Large reservoirs and dams provide suitable sites for floating solar projects.

Analyze the role of such large-scale renewable energy projects in achieving India's climate goals and energy security.
Examine the environmental and economic benefits of transitioning to clean energy sources in Southern India.
Evaluate the challenges in developing and integrating floating solar projects into the national grid and suggest solutions.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Green Hydrogen Mission, consider the following statements:
1. Its target is to achieve 5 million metric tonnes (MMT) of green hydrogen production capacity annually by 2030.
2. The mission provides Production Linked Incentives (PLI) solely for green hydrogen production, not for electrolyzer manufacturing.
3. It aims to reduce cumulative fossil fuel imports by over ₹1 lakh crore by 2030.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The mission targets 5 MMT of green hydrogen production capacity annually by 2030. Statement 2 is incorrect: The mission provides PLI for both electrolyzer manufacturing and green hydrogen production. Statement 3 is correct: The mission aims for a cumulative reduction in fossil fuel imports by over ₹1 lakh crore by 2030.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is a non-permanent constitutional body established under Article 263 of the Indian Constitution, with the Prime Minister as its Chairman?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Inter-State Council is a non-permanent constitutional body established under Article 263 of the Indian Constitution by the President of India. The Prime Minister is the Chairman of the Inter-State Council. National Development Council and NITI Aayog are extra-constitutional bodies, and Zonal Councils are statutory bodies.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Indus Water Treaty (IWT):
1. It was signed between India and Pakistan in 1960, brokered by the World Bank.
2. The treaty allocates the waters of the Eastern Rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab) to India.
3. The Permanent Indus Commission (PIC) is a bilateral commission for implementation and dispute resolution under the treaty.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The IWT was signed in 1960, brokered by the World Bank. Statement 2 is incorrect: The treaty allocates the Eastern Rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej) to India, and the Western Rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab) to Pakistan. Statement 3 is correct: The PIC is indeed a bilateral body for the treaty's implementation and dispute resolution.

prelimsMocks.question: The 'NIPUN Bharat' mission, recently in news, is associated with which of the following objectives?
options: (a) Promoting digital literacy among rural women.
(b) Achieving universal foundational literacy and numeracy for all children by Grade 3.
(c) Enhancing skilled workforce in the manufacturing sector.
(d) Providing financial assistance to startups in Tier-2 cities.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Achieving universal foundational literacy and numeracy for all children by Grade 3.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The 'NIPUN Bharat' mission is a flagship initiative under the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, launched with the aim of achieving universal foundational literacy and numeracy (FLN) for all children by the end of Grade 3 by the year 2026-27.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following statements best describes 'Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI)' in the Indian context?
options: (a) A network of government-owned data centers for secure data storage.
(b) A set of open, interoperable digital systems and platforms like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker.
(c) Infrastructure for high-speed internet connectivity in public spaces.
(d) A regulatory framework for digital currency and blockchain technology.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) A set of open, interoperable digital systems and platforms like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) in the Indian context refers to the shared digital systems and platforms like Aadhaar (digital identity), UPI (payments), and DigiLocker (data exchange) that enable the delivery of public and private services in a scalable, interoperable, and cost-effective manner. This is often collectively called the 'India Stack'.

GS1.headline: Government Launches 'Nari Shakti Udyam Yojana' to Boost Women Entrepreneurship and Self-Reliance
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development, in collaboration with SIDBI, launched the 'Nari Shakti Udyam Yojana', a new scheme aimed at empowering women entrepreneurs across India.
• The scheme provides financial assistance, mentorship, skill development training, and market linkages to women-led micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSMEs).
• It focuses on promoting entrepreneurship in both traditional and non-traditional sectors, with a special emphasis on digital literacy and e-commerce platforms.
• The initiative seeks to address gender disparities in business ownership and contribute to women's economic independence and overall societal development.
• Beneficiaries will receive subsidized loans, incubation support, and access to a dedicated online portal for resources and networking.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Reinforces Call for Greater Transparency in Electoral Funding, Directs EC on Disclosure Norms
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a significant ruling, reiterated the imperative for enhanced transparency in political funding and directed the Election Commission of India (ECI) to ensure stricter disclosure norms for political parties.
• The judgment emphasized that the right to information of the voter extends to knowing the source of funding for political parties, crucial for a healthy democracy.
• While not overturning previous judgments on electoral bonds, the court urged the ECI to proactively publish data and investigate non-compliance with existing disclosure requirements.
• This ruling comes amidst ongoing debates about the opaqueness of electoral financing and its impact on free and fair elections.
• The court highlighted the need for legislative action to address the structural issues in electoral funding, while also leveraging existing legal frameworks for greater accountability.

• Discussions centered on bolstering physical and digital connectivity, including infrastructure projects and collaboration in digital public infrastructure.
• A joint statement highlighted renewed emphasis on maritime security cooperation, freedom of navigation, and adherence to international law in the Indo-Pacific region.
• Economic engagement was a major agenda point, with calls for boosting trade and investment, and exploring new avenues for supply chain resilience.
• India reiterated its 'Act East' policy and its vision for a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, aligning with ASEAN's centrality.

• Key discussions included the implementation of uniform education policies, health infrastructure development, and efficient disaster management strategies.
• The meeting aimed to foster better coordination between the Union and State governments on various legislative and administrative matters.
• States raised concerns regarding fiscal federalism, resource allocation, and the need for greater autonomy in certain policy domains.
• Recommendations are expected to be forwarded to the Union government to address inter-state disputes and streamline the implementation of national programs.
